report_name: McGraw Hill Confirms Breach of 13.5 Million Accounts; ShinyHunters Claims Attack via Salesforce Misconfiguration

threat_actors.name: ShinyHunters
threat_actors.description: A well-known cybercriminal group specializing in large-scale data breaches, often by exploiting misconfigured cloud services. They claimed responsibility for the McGraw Hill breach, exfiltrated over 100GB of data, and leaked it on a dark web forum after a ransom was not paid.
threat_actors.goals: Data exfiltration for extortion and subsequent leaking on dark web forums.

attack_patterns.name: Vulnerability Scanning
attack_patterns.description: ShinyHunters likely used scanning tools to discover misconfigured public-facing Salesforce sites or community pages that allowed unauthorized access.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Cloud Storage
attack_patterns.description: After discovering the misconfigured Salesforce instance, ShinyHunters exfiltrated a dataset of over 100GB containing names, phone numbers, physical addresses, and unique email addresses.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: The attack exploited overly permissive access rights granted to unauthenticated guest user profiles on a Salesforce Experience Cloud site, allowing unauthorized access to the underlying data.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over Unencrypted Non-C2 Protocol
attack_patterns.description: ShinyHunters exfiltrated a 100GB dataset from the compromised cloud environment to their own infrastructure.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: The attack likely exploited publicly exposed Salesforce API endpoints that did not enforce proper authentication or authorization checks, allowing unauthorized data access.

identities.name: McGraw Hill
identities.description: Educational publishing and technology company that was the primary victim of the data breach, losing 13.5 million user records due to a misconfiguration on a third-party Salesforce platform.

identities.name: Salesforce
identities.description: Third-party CRM provider whose cloud platform was misconfigured, leading to the McGraw Hill data breach. The point of failure was external to McGraw Hill's core infrastructure.

identities.name: Have I Been Pwned
identities.description: Data breach notification service that ingested the leaked McGraw Hill dataset and identified 13.5 million unique email addresses affected by the incident.

infrastructure.name: ShinyHunters Dark Web Leak Site
infrastructure.description: A dark web portal used by ShinyHunters to post threats, demand ransoms, and leak stolen data, including the 100GB dataset from McGraw Hill.

indicators.name: Salesforce SOQL API Query
indicators.pattern:
title: Salesforce SOQL API Query
id: 2b8c1f3a-9d4e-4b1f-8c2a-5d7e6f1a0b3c
status: experimental
description: Detects queries to the Salesforce SOQL API endpoint, which could be used for unauthorized data exfiltration from misconfigured instances.
references:
  - https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/data-breach-at-edtech-giant-mcgraw-hill-affects-135-million-accounts/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-17
tags:
  - attack.t1530
  - attack.collection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate application usage of the Salesforce API. This rule is best used to detect anomalous sources or volumes.
level: medium
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    c-uri|contains|all:
      - '/services/data/'
      - '/query?q='
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects queries to the Salesforce SOQL API endpoint, which could be used for unauthorized data exfiltration from misconfigured instances as seen in the McGraw Hill breach.
